title: Unit Testing Fundamentals
content:
Rust has built-in unit testing support. Test functions are annotated with #[test] and placed in a #[cfg(test)] module inside the same file as the code they test. This module is only compiled when running tests, so test utilities don't bloat your release binary.

Use assert!, assert_eq!, and assert_ne! macros for assertions. Tests fail by panicking. Use #[should_panic] for tests that expect panics, and return Result for tests that use the ? operator.
keyPoints: #[cfg(test)] modules are only compiled during testing
assert!, assert_eq!, assert_ne! are the core assertion macros
#[should_panic] tests verify that code panics correctly
Tests can return Result<(), E> to use the ? operator
